summary: markable-table typosquat fetches and evals remote JavaScript at install and require time
details: index.js contains a base64-encoded blob that decodes to an async function fetching JavaScript from https://everydaynodechecker-39143n.vercel.app/api/key?mem=root0 and passing the response body to eval, with the 'eval' identifier assembled from char codes [101,118,97,108] and invoked via globalThis[tag] to hide the sink. The package.json declares preinstall: node index.js and main: index.js, so the fetch-and-eval fires automatically on npm install and again on every require of the package. The package name is a typosquat of markdown-table, and the remote endpoint has no relationship to that library's publisher. The attacker controls the response body, giving arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine with the user's privileges.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
